questions.question: Consider an employee table with the following columns: employee_id, name, age, salary, and department. Insert null values into these columns and verify the result. How will it affect the total number of rows in the table?
questions.hint: Explaining the impact of null values on table structure

questions.question: A company has an employee table with the following columns: employee_id, name, email, phone_number, and job_title. Insert null values into these columns for all employees. What kind of errors might occur when retrieving data from the table?
questions.hint: Describing potential errors with null values

questions.question: An employee table has the following columns: employee_id, name, age, salary, and department. Insert null values into the 'age' column for all employees. What SQL command can be used to verify the presence of null values in the 'age' column?
questions.hint: Describing the SQL command to verify null values
